items.q: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. What is the relationship between co-interior (allied) angles?
items.misconception: They are equal to each other, like alternate angles.
items.random_wrong: They add up to 360 degrees because they make a full turn.
items.why: Students lump all parallel-line angle relationships into 'equal' (because corresponding and alternate are equal), missing that co-interior angles sum to 180.

items.correct: 18 — division and multiplication share the same precedence; work left to right (6 x 3).
items.misconception: 2, because in BIDMAS multiplication comes before division (12 / 6).
items.random_wrong: 7, because you do 12 / 2 = 6 and then add 3 with the multiplication symbol meaning 'and'.
items.why: Students treat BIDMAS as a strict order (M before D), missing that multiplication and division have equal priority and are evaluated left to right.

items.q: On a distance-time graph, what does a horizontal line mean?
items.correct: The object is stationary — distance is not changing, so speed is zero.
items.misconception: The object is moving at a constant speed.
items.why: Students confuse distance-time graphs with speed-time graphs; a horizontal line on the latter would mean constant speed.
It's 180°. “360°” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Equal. “Supplementary” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The real figure is 115. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
